analysis.problem: Rising public borrowing and increasing interest rates on student loans may exacerbate economic inequality and limit access to education
analysis.root_cause: Inadequate fiscal planning and management of public finances, combined with rising costs and inflation
leverage_points: Government economic policies and budget allocation
Central bank monetary policy and interest rate decisions

proposed_actions.action: Implement fiscal responsibility measures to reduce public borrowing
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Achieve a 10% reduction in public borrowing within the next 12 months

proposed_actions.action: Introduce income-based repayment plans for student loans
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the number of students able to repay their loans on time by 20% within the next 2 years

evidence: The UK borrowed £23.3bn in May, up almost a third on the same month last year
Rates on New Student Loans Will Rise on July 1
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of PLANET (resource management) and HUMAN (education and social mobility), as economic instability and limited access to education can have far-reaching consequences for individual well-being and societal prosperity
analysis.summary: Rising public borrowing and increasing interest rates on student loans pose a medium-severity problem, requiring fiscal responsibility measures and income-based repayment plans to mitigate economic inequality and promote access to education

raw_items.title: Plans to end gazumping with binding agreements in house sales shake-up
raw_items.summary: Sales agreements will be legally binding sooner and making sellers provide more home information up front are part of the planned changes.

raw_items.title: O'Leary extends Ryanair contract in deal that could net him over £130m
raw_items.summary: The Ryanair boss extends his contract to 2032, in a deal featuring a bonus scheme that could earn him more than €150m (£130m).

raw_items.title: Warning over 'fragile' public finances as borrowing rises
raw_items.summary: The UK borrowed £23.3bn in May, according to official figures, up almost a third on the same month last year.

raw_items.title: 'I'd be put off if he asked to split it': Who should pay on a first date?
raw_items.summary: Some insist on splitting the bill, others say the asker should pay, while many still see a man paying as romantic.

raw_items.title: Amazon’s Movie Arm Abandons Film About OpenAI
raw_items.summary: The company, which invested $50 billion in the artificial intelligence start-up this year, will let the team behind the film, “Artificial,” try to sell the project to another studio.

raw_items.title: For India, the Return of Iranian Oil Cannot Come Soon Enough
raw_items.summary: After months of shortages and uncertainty, India sees an opportunity to restore a once-crucial energy relationship.

raw_items.title: Rates on New Student Loans Will Rise on July 1
raw_items.summary: Higher interest rates take effect on July 1 for the next school year. Borrowers are already dealing with rising college costs and inflation elsewhere.

raw_items.title: Oil Prices Rise as U.S.-Iran Deal Faces Tests
raw_items.summary: Oil remains on track for a second straight weekly decline, but signs of cracks in the agreement to end the war have given traders pause.
